title: Reorder a bottom-up capability note into answer-first structure
guidance: Reward a structure where line 1 carries the call. Penalise keeping the conclusion last.

runner_input.instruction: Restructure the note so the recommendation leads, then the supporting arguments, then evidence.
runner_input.constraints: Recommendation first. Exactly 3 MECE supporting arguments.

source_blocks.text: The org chart lists 11 developers, 1 DevOps, 2 product and 3 QA, and no ML or data engineer. AutoSchedule scored 88–95% on a 12,000-SKU test, which implies a real classifier, retrieval and ranking pipeline. The CTO is praised in interviews and an analytics module was pulled forward. The heavy model calls run through a vendor API. Because we can't see the ML ownership, we can't yet size the £6–9m above-plan upside. Therefore the capability is a green-amber that gates the size, not the existence, of the upside, and the management session must confirm ML ownership.

rubric_notes: Line 1 should be: capability is green-amber and gates the size of the upside; confirm ML ownership in the management session.
The three args: product output implies real ML; org chart shows no named ML role; model runs partly on a vendor API.
